gaps: The CourtListener MCP server did not come up; a health check or automatic REST fallback guidance in the prompt would make this failure mode cheaper.
Panel composition for argued CA10 appeals is absent from the snapshot and the audio metadata; a provisioned panel list would enable per-judge votes.
Corpus priors and the statpack have almost no circuit-appeal coverage (CA10: 34 resolved, cert-flavored labels), so appeal-disposition cells get no useful corpus base rate.
No filed-document text was provisioned for this appellate cell; the appellant/appellee briefs would have sharpened the issue framing more than the district docket alone.
helpful: The provisioned snapshot's audio/cluster links made it easy to locate the case and confirm posture.
CourtListener RECAP plain_text of the district court's summary-judgment order was the decisive input.
metrics/statpack.md as a committed file was instantly readable even with the corpus blob remote.
notes: Used the CourtListener REST API v4 directly (with the provisioned token) as a stand-in for the unavailable MCP server; all calls are listed in retrieval.md. fedcourts query worked via the ranged backend but returned nothing topically relevant for a CA10 EMTALA appeal.
tools_used: CourtListener REST API v4 (audio, search, docket-entries, recap-documents)
